title: Street Urchin
description: Vin is summoned to meet her crewleader Camon, who is rough with her. She unconsciously uses her Luck—a form of Allomantic Soothing—to placate him. Camon pretends to be Lord Jedue, a nobleman desperately seeking new contracts, and prepares to scam the Steel Ministry. Vin uses her Luck on the obligator Prelan Laird so he won't dismiss Camon's pleas.

title: The Ministry Scam
description: Camon, as Lord Jedue, takes Vin to the Canton of Finance to scam the Ministry. Vin uses her Luck on the High Prelan, who gives Camon three thousand boxings. Kelsier and Dockson sit in the waiting room and notice Vin's Allomantic pulses. Afterward, they spot a Steel Inquisitor tailing Camon's retinue.

title: Recruited
description: Kelsier pitches his plan to overthrow the Final Empire to the crew at Clubs' carpentry shop. The crew agrees—except Clubs, who walks out before eventually returning. Kelsier reveals to the others that Vin is a Mistborn, then tells them about and shows them the Eleventh Metal.

title: Mistborn Training
description: Kelsier takes Vin out for her first night of training, giving her a Mistcloak. He teaches her about the eight basic metals—tin, pewter, iron, steel, zinc, brass, copper, and bronze—ending the night at the Luthadel city wall.

title: The Renoux Estate
description: Vin and Kelsier go over the Luthadel walls into the night wilderness, where Vin encounters a mistwraith for the first time. On the main road, Sazed is waiting with a carriage and takes them to Fellise. On the way, Kelsier offers Vin the three thousand boxings and the chance to leave the crew. She takes the money but stays because she wants to see how things turn out. At Lord Renoux's estate, Renoux proposes that Vin play the part of Lady Valette, a daughter of a favored cousin, to be introduced to the other noble houses.

title: Training with Sazed
description: Vin and Kelsier spar in a training match at the Renoux estate. Afterward, Vin gets a haircut and is quizzed over her noble cover by Sazed, who also tells her about Trelagism—the fifth religion he has pitched to her. Kelsier and Sazed later talk in private about Vin's progress, with Kelsier declaring she will attend a ball at Keep Venture at the end of the week.

title: Return to the Crew
description: Vin returns to Luthadel from Fellise to learn about metals from the crew's Mistings, starting with Breeze who teaches her about Soothing. Hammond arrives and debates the morality of overthrowing the Final Empire as they wait for Kelsier to give a speech.

title: The Warehouse Rally
description: Vin attends a secret rally in the warehouse district where Kelsier preaches revolution to gathered skaa workers, with Breeze and his Soothers and Rioters manipulating the crowd. Afterward, Kelsier holds a progress meeting at Clubs' shop. Vin and Lestibournes talk about Marsh. The meeting ends when one of Hammond's soldiers reports the Steel Ministry has discovered Camon's old lair.

title: Camon's Lair Massacre
description: Vin and the crew investigate the destroyed lair, finding corpses throughout. Among them, Vin notices Ulef—someone had ripped his rib cage apart with bare hands. Sazed recites a prayer of the Cazzi religion off to the side. Milev's body is found tied to a chair, clearly tortured. After leaving, Kelsier finds Camon's corpse hung from a hook rammed through his mouth, wrists and ankles bound and body tortured.

title: First Ball at Keep Venture
description: Vin heads off to her first ball at Keep Venture, Sazed driving the carriage. Kelsier drops in while the carriage is en route, gives Vin some advice, then jumps back into the mists. Vin arrives and is introduced as Lady Valette Renoux. During dinner she sees her father across the room—Reen had identified him to her when she was younger. She declines dancing invitations from various nobles, then takes a break upstairs on the balcony, where she has her first conversation with Elend Venture, which shocks Sazed when he hears about it.

title: Return to Fellise
description: Sazed questions Vin about her interaction with Elend at the Renoux estate. Vin's true personality is beginning to emerge—neither the timid crewmember nor Lady Valette. Kelsier learns that Vin's father was at the ball, then heads out into the mists again.

title: Into Kredik Shaw
description: Kelsier leads Vin into Kredik Shaw, and they fight their way toward the building the Lord Ruler visits every three days. Vin kills for the first time. Three Steel Inquisitors close in on them. To defend herself from sharpened metal triangles being Pushed at her, Vin grabs a random leather-bound book. Burning atium, she manages a temporary escape before an Inquisitor impales her in the side with an obsidian-bladed axe. Vin escapes Kredik Shaw still clutching the book, but as she loses consciousness, something distracts the pursuing Inquisitor and takes her into a protective embrace—it wasn't Kelsier.

title: Wounded
description: The crew argues about Kelsier's decision to take Vin to Kredik Shaw. Before they can go out to search for her, Sazed arrives carrying Vin. She is fed pewter to help her heal from her wounds. Sazed operates on her, stitching her back together, and Ham carries her to a room to recover. Kelsier and Dockson notice the leather-bound book she brought back, and Sazed identifies it as written in Khlenni—the pre-Ascension language of the Lord Ruler's homeland.

title: Recovery at Clubs' Shop
description: Vin awakens after two weeks of rest at Clubs' shop. Spook has been watching over her and tells her how Kelsier gave him his new nickname. While talking with Dockson, Vin realizes there is something very unusual about Sazed.

title: Return to Fellise
description: After a few more days of rest, Vin returns to the Renoux estate in Fellise. She speaks with Sazed, who explains what Keepers are and reveals that all Terrisman stewards are eunuchs.

title: The Garden of Mansion Renoux
description: Vin laments a four-week recovery full of boredom at the Renoux estate. Members of the crew arrive to discuss updates. Vin convinces Kelsier to let her attend an upcoming ball. Later, Vin and Kelsier talk about Mare, his motivations, and he explains Snapping—the traumatic event that awakens Allomantic powers.

title: Ball at Keep Elariel
description: Vin attends a ball at Keep Elariel with Sazed. Elend Venture joins her at a table, sitting and reading as usual. He compliments her dress, leaving her jaw-dropped. She dances with various nobles and asks about Elend, learning about Lady Shan Elariel who has spoken about Vin. After much dancing, Vin is required to meet with Shan Elariel. During the conversation, Vin realizes that Shan can Soothe. Vin notices Shan's Terrisman poking through Elend's books—left unattended at her table—so she excuses herself and returns. She scans through a small book that is practically treasonous in its content. Elend returns, gathers his books, and leaves, met on the way by two friends. Sazed identifies them as Jastes Lekal and a Hasting—surprising, since Lekal and Hasting are political rivals of Venture. As Vin leaves the ball, she witnesses a city guard slit the throat of a young skaa kitchen boy in the mists.

title: Returning to Fellise
description: Kelsier returns to Mansion Renoux via the Spikeway and meets with Vin, Sazed, and Renoux. He lectures Vin about noblemen, insisting they don't care about the skaa and that she should keep away from Elend Venture.

title: Seeking Lessons
description: Vin returns from Luthadel to see weapons being crated up in front of Renoux's mansion, to be shipped out for the rebellion. Marsh arrives soon after and teaches her about Seeking—using bronze to sense other Allomancers burning metals—as well as additional details about Allomancy in general.

title: The Logbook
description: Vin reads from her copy of the Lord Ruler's logbook at the Renoux estate when Spook arrives. He gives her a handkerchief—Sazed later explains that this is how a young man signals he would like to seriously court a lady. While waiting for Dockson, Vin talks to Sazed about the Terrisman in the journal, and Sazed reveals the secrets of Feruchemy and how the Keepers were formed to remember everything after the Terris religion was lost. Vin then speaks with Dockson, who tells her how a woman he loved was taken by Lord Devinshae and killed the next morning, insisting all noblemen are like that.

title: Ball at Keep Hasting
description: Vin attends a ball at Keep Hasting, pleased to hear that Kelsier's efforts at stoking tension among the Houses are working. She also learns that the Inquisitors are tracking down skaa thieves more frequently. Lady Shan approaches and tells Vin to search through Elend's books the next time she sees him and report back. As Vin waits for Sazed, she finds Elend and asks him whether he has ever slept with a skaa woman. Elend confesses that his father forced him to when he was thirteen, and the woman was killed afterward, but he never did it again. Elend gives Vin a handkerchief, signaling his wish to court her. After Elend leaves with his friends, Vin follows them using Allomancy to a tower where they discuss the Lord Ruler's faults and what their generation should do about it. Kelsier appears suddenly and dismisses the group's ideas as typical youthful idealism that changes nothing.

title: Crew Meeting
description: Vin and Kelsier arrive at Clubs' shop where many of the crew are gathered. Kelsier discusses the status of the mission, and Vin realizes how much she loves her new life—no longer hiding in the shadows. Kelsier, Spook, Ham, and Clubs share a late-night drink.

title: The Garrison Visit
description: Vin changes into her urchin clothes and goes out with Ham to visit the Luthadel Garrison to learn about pewter. Ham explains that many of the soldiers are ordinary skaa who just want a normal living. At the garrison, they learn from a guard named Sertes that the Luthadel Garrison is mobilizing—a skaa rebellion has attacked the Holstep Garrison to the north.

title: Urgent News
description: At Clubs' shop, Ham returns with news that the Luthadel Garrison is moving north toward Holstep. Kelsier makes Vin swallow five pewter beads so they can run north to warn the rebel army.

title: The Pewter Drag
description: Vin and Kelsier run for sixteen hours, burning pewter continuously in a desperate pewter drag. On the way, they encounter the remains of the rebel army, surrounded and slaughtered by the Valtroux Garrison. Kelsier wants to join and save who he can, but Vin convinces him they are not invincible and instead they hurry on to the Arguois Caverns, where they find 2,000 soldiers still alive thanks to Mennis keeping them prepared to leave at a moment's notice.

title: Recovery
description: Vin lies in bed at Clubs' shop, exhausted. It took her and Kelsier two weeks to return to Luthadel. Kelsier bursts in, and Breeze blames him for chasing fame and glory, declaring the job dead. Kelsier admits the loss of the rebel army is a setback but refuses to give up.

title: The Executions
description: Spook announces executions at the Fountain Square. The crew head to the square, bribing someone for rooftop access. Vin identifies her father to Kelsier—an obligator called Tevidian, Lord Prelan and the highest-ranked obligator, even above the Inquisitors. They watch as women and children are dragged from a prison cart and executed, the fountain running red with blood. Kelsier tells the crew: 'This is our enemy. There is no quarter here, no walking away.'

title: Planning Next Moves
description: The crew returns to Clubs' shop. Kelsier, Vin, and the rest—Dockson, Ham, Breeze, Spook—begin replanning how to move forward and start a house war between the nobility. Sazed arrives with a letter from Marsh containing instructions on where to meet.

title: Meeting Marsh
description: Kelsier and Vin head to an abandoned building in the Twists to meet Marsh. While waiting, Kelsier talks about gold—the ninth Allomantic metal that lets a user see an alternative past version of themselves. Marsh arrives bearing the tattoos of a low-ranked obligator from the Canton of Inquisition. He reveals that the Ministry places Soothers in skaa regions under constant Soothing, hidden by Smokers. Marsh also confirms that Inquisitors can die of old age.

title: Ball at Keep Lekal
description: Vin attends a ball at Keep Lekal and begins spreading rumors to ignite the House Wars. She talks with Lady Kliss, gossiping that House Hastings are relocating resources to their plantation in the Southern Dominance. Elend surprises her—he says he wants to kiss her, but Vin insists he doesn't truly know her. They take a stroll instead, and Elend reveals that House Venture's fortune comes entirely from mining atium at the Pits of Hathsin, trusting her completely. He also mentions that the Tekiels were killed by House Hasting, and tries to convince Valette to leave Luthadel.

title: The Last Translation
description: Vin reads the last of Sazed's translations of the logbook at Mansion Renoux and is disappointed there isn't any more. Sazed explains Feruchemy to her in more detail, and she tries burning one of his pewterminds. Later, Kelsier joins the crew at Renoux's estate to go over a detailed map provided by Marsh. They discover that Theron's crew was recently hit by the Ministry, and Vin suspects the Inquisitors are still on her trail.

title: The Venture Ball
description: Vin attends the last house ball at Keep Venture with Sazed. The partygoers are finalizing alliances—Lady Valette is considered too neutral. Lady Kliss reveals herself as an informant and tries to blackmail House Renoux. In exchange for a sapphire necklace, Kliss tells Vin that Elariel assassins will try to kill Elend that evening, and that Straff has reduced the guards around Elend's room. Using emotional Allomancy, Vin forces Kliss to reveal the full plan. She destroys a stained glass window to escape the keep. Outside, she fights off two Thugs, two Coinshots, and two Mistborn—one of whom is Shan Elariel. Vin tricks Shan by extinguishing her atium early and kills her.

title: Aftermath of the Duel
description: Vin bursts into Clubs' kitchen bandaged and in tatters. Kelsier scolds her for saving Elend, but Vin screams back that skaa and noblemen are no different—Elend is a good man. On the rooftop afterward, Vin tells Kelsier about House Venture's administration of the Pits and shares her belief that Inquisitors can see through copperclouds. Kelsier tests her theory and proves her correct—a major discovery.

title: Scouting the Rooftops
description: Vin and Spook scout from the rooftop above Clubs' shop. Spook teaches Vin tricks about using tin—it's not just about enhancing senses but about sorting out distractions. Later, Kelsier and Vin find Marsh's body, drained of all blood—a lifeless shell. Back at Clubs' shop, they discover a letter from Marsh explaining that the Inquisitors would probably find him.

title: The Backup Lair
description: The crew relocates to a dank backup cellar safehouse near Clubs' shop, just three rooms. Kelsier returns and announces he destroyed all the atium at the Pits of Hathsin. Spook is keeping watch as a Tineye.

title: The Inquisitor Fight
description: Everyone follows Kelsier to Fountain Square. They spot Spook and Lord Renoux in one of the carts being taken to execution. Kelsier fights an Inquisitor in the square. Elend sees the Renoux servants caged and screams after Valette, but cannot find her.

title: The Survivor's Death
description: Kelsier fights the Inquisitor in an all-out battle at Fountain Square, both burning atium. Vin observes from above—Kelsier is truly a master of Push and Pull, moving metal objects through the air like an expert. He kills the Inquisitor and the surrounding skaa begin chanting 'The Survivor of Hathsin.' The Lord Ruler arrives in a black carriage. One of Ham's thugs rams a spear through the Lord Ruler—he continues walking. Two spears—still walking. The Lord Ruler slaps Kelsier, pulls a spear from his own body, and rams it through Kelsier. Vin jumps down from above and holds Kelsier as he dies.

title: The Survivor's Legacy
description: Vin returns to the backup safehouse near Clubs' shop. She is furious that Kelsier wasn't truthful—he had planned to die and abandon her. Sazed offers comforting words. Outside, skaa gather and speak of the Lord of the Mists. At a nearby warehouse, the crew sees the kandra that has taken Kelsier's form. Kelsier left letters to each crew member with instructions for taking the city. Vin's letter says he never figured out the Eleventh Metal. She decides she will find out what the Lord Ruler is hiding.

title: Into Kredik Shaw
description: Vin walks quietly toward Kredik Shaw, encouraging the guards outside to join the rebellion. Inside, she kills two Inquisitors guarding an inner entrance, discovering that pewter dust disrupts their vision. In the chamber beyond sits an old man in a chair. An Inquisitor grabs Vin by the neck—the old man speaks, and the Inquisitors address him as the Lord Ruler. But Vin is confused: the Lord Ruler at Fountain Square was a young man. She burns the Eleventh Metal and sees two ghostly shapes beside the old man—one resembles the young Lord Ruler, but slightly older.

title: Rescue and Revelation
description: The Inquisitors force Vin to burn a metal that strips away all her other metals. She is brought before the Lord Ruler on his elevated throne—the young version, the man who killed Kelsier. Vin is questioned about her father. Tevidian, Vin's father and highest-ranked obligator, is killed by the Inquisitors when the Lord Ruler learns the truth. Vin discovers that Reen never sold her out—he endured torture and told the Inquisitors she had died of starvation long ago. Sazed is brought to her cell, then unleashes stored Feruchemical strength to burst the door open. Elend and six soldiers charge in. Vin finds her Mistcloak and a vial of metals in a trunk, swallows them, and kills the remaining guard. The secret is out—Elend now knows Vin is a Mistborn. She grabs his head and kisses him, then turns to go kill the Lord Ruler.

title: Slayer of the Lord Ruler
description: Vin jumps through the spires of Kredik Shaw to the top. The Inquisitor Kar grabs her by the neck, but a second Inquisitor approaches and pulls a metal spike from Kar's neck, killing him instantly. The second Inquisitor is Marsh—alive, with eleven spikes in his body. He has killed all the other Inquisitors. The Lord Ruler's true name is Rashek—he murdered the Hero of Ages and took his place. Vin pulls on the Lord Ruler's metal bracelets and they drop. Without his Feruchemical youth he becomes a weak, pathetic old man. Sazed appears with a vial of metals. Vin rams a spear directly through the Lord Ruler's heart. A thousand years of oppression end.

title: A New Era
description: Sazed, Marsh, and Vin stand on a building near the edge of the skaa slum. Sazed explains that the Lord Ruler was both a Feruchemist and an Allomancer—the bracelets stored his youth. Marsh describes his survival and transformation: eleven metal spikes, two through his eyes, eight in his chest, one in the back binding them together. The process to create an Inquisitor is messy. He also speaks of the kandra—creatures evolved from mistwraiths, who now see Vin as their master.

title: Vin and Elend
description: Vin secretly watches Elend from the mists outside Keep Venture. His meeting with skaa and noble leaders went well—everyone has accepted him as king after a tremendous speech that even Sazed couldn't memorize in full without his metals. Elend sits writing the new code of law for the kingdom. Vin almost decides not to go to him, but remembers that Reen never gave her up to the Inquisitors—there are people who truly love her. With that memory she dashes toward Elend and they hold each other.

title: City Guardian
description: Vin patrols the walls of Luthadel each night, watching for threats as three armies slowly converge on the newly free city.

title: Ambush in the Mists
description: Vin is ambushed by eight Allomancers south of Keep Venture near Kredik Shaw. A mysterious Mistborn—the Watcher—intervenes and helps her kill or scatter them. She suspects the assassins were sent by one of the besieging armies.

title: The Mist Spirit
description: Vin watches Keep Venture from a rooftop and encounters the mist spirit—a translucent figure that points toward something unseen. She returns to Elend's study, where Ham arrives with news that the assassins were likely sent by King Cett.

title: OreSeur Reborn
description: Vin walks through Luthadel's Kenton Street market and buys a wolfhound, which she gives to OreSeur the kandra as his new body. She discovers a new Allomantic metal—duralumin—and returns to Keep Venture to test its power.

title: Sparring in the Yard
description: Vin spars with Ham in Keep Venture's courtyard, testing her combat skills while OreSeur watches in his new wolfhound form. Spook returns to the crew from his scouting mission.

title: Chasing the Watcher
description: Vin patrols Luthadel and chases the Watcher—the mysterious Mistborn—across the city's rooftops. The pursuit ends near Fountain Square, where she experiments with burning duralumin and discovers its devastating enhancing effect.

title: Guarding the Assembly
description: Vin and Ham guard Elend as he addresses the Assembly. An unknown Terriswoman is in the audience. During the meeting, news arrives that a second army—King Cett's forces—has appeared outside Luthadel.

title: Saving Breeze
description: Vin joins Elend and Clubs on the city wall to scout Cett's army. When Breeze is endangered below, Vin uses a duralumin-enhanced Steelpush to save him—launching a coin with terrifying force. Back at Keep Venture, the crew discovers kandra bones, raising fears of an impostor.

title: The Spirit Returns
description: Vin notices the mists arriving earlier each evening and searches for the mist spirit, finding it in an abandoned apartment block. She falls from a window during the encounter. Later, Elend gathers the crew at Keep Venture for a war council, and Tindwyl requests a private audience with him.

title: Tindwyl Arrives
description: Tindwyl begins tutoring Elend in kingship at Keep Venture. Vin distrusts the forceful Terriswoman but watches Elend slowly begin to change under her guidance.

title: The Logbook Search
description: Vin pulls apart Alendi's logbook at Keep Venture, searching for references to the mist spirit and the Deepness. She thinks the creature following her is the same one that followed Alendi centuries ago.

title: Dueling the Watcher
description: After meeting Straff's messenger—Zane, the Watcher. Vin goes on patrol with OreSeur. She confronts Zane and they end up in a fierce steel-pushing contest at Keep Hasting. Zane tells her that Mistborn belong in the mists, not with nobles.

title: War Council
description: Vin observes as Elend, Dockson, Hammond, Clubs and Breeze discuss the plan to parlay with Straff at Keep Venture. She uses bronze to eliminate Breeze as the kandra impostor suspect. Lord Cett's daughter Allrianne arrives seeking asylum and Breeze.

title: Allrianne's Arrival
description: Allrianne Cett arrives at the palace and embraces Breeze, surprising everyone. Elend offers her hospitality. Dockson proposes using Allrianne as a bargaining chip against Lord Cett.

title: Political Tensions
description: As the siege tightens, Vin watches Elend struggle with the Assembly and the demands of governance while she hunts Zane through the mists.

title: Night Watch
description: Vin stands guard outside Elend's balcony and burns bronze, detecting the mist spirit and a distant source of Allomantic power. Zane finds her at Keep Venture and challenges her, asking what she would do without repercussions. She answers that she would kill the enemy armies' leaders.

title: Shopping and Skirmish
description: Tindwyl takes Vin dress shopping on Kenton Street along with Allrianne, Spook, and OreSeur. Outside the shop, Vin speaks to members of the Church of the Survivor. Warning drums sound. Straff's army launches a feint attack on the city walls and Vin rushes to help defend.

title: Parley with Straff
description: Vin accompanies Elend to Straff Venture's war camp north of the city, where she demonstrates her terrifying power to intimidate the would-be conqueror.

title: The Bluff
description: While Vin listens from outside the tent, Straff threatens to kill Elend during the parley. Zane appears. Vin and Elend's bluff succeeds. Straff lets them leave. On the road back to Luthadel, Elend receives a note: the Assembly has voted no confidence and he is no longer king.

title: Return from Parley
description: After the tense encounter at Straff's camp, Vin returns to Keep Venture, haunted by Zane's whispers and her growing doubts about her role.

title: Insomnia and Insight
description: Vin and OreSeur sit at Keep Hasting in the night mists. They discuss Zane's character and Vin's growing insomnia. OreSeur says he does not want to change bodies often. Vin has a flash of insight about the true nature of the Deepness.

title: The Deepness Theory
description: Vin enters Keep Venture through Sazed's window and reads his transcription of the Conventical rubbings. She proposes that the mists are the Deepness - that permanent daytime mists could cause famine. She tells Sazed she has seen the same creature that followed the Hero of Ages.

title: The Assembly Crisis
description: Vin watches from the gallery as the Assembly votes to depose Elend and invite Lord Penrod to lead, shattering the democratic experiment.

title: Raid on Cett
description: Vin launches a devastating solo assault on Cett's forces quartered at Keep Hasting, driving his army from the city in a display of Mistborn power.

title: Duel with Zane
description: Near Keep Hasting, Vin duels Zane Venture in a brutal Mistborn battle, ultimately killing him to protect those she loves.

title: Exile
description: Vin and Elend depart Luthadel through the Tin Gate, exiled by Penrod's new government, heading north toward Terris with a small escort.

title: Exodus North
description: The small party travels toward Terris, but Vin senses something pulling her back—the Well of Ascension is not in the mountains after all.

title: Return to Battle
description: Vin makes a desperate pewter drag back to Luthadel as koloss breach the walls, arriving in time to take control of the creatures and save the city.

title: Searching for the Well
description: With Luthadel saved but battered, Vin searches beneath Kredik Shaw for the Well of Ascension, following the mist spirit's guidance deeper underground.

title: The Well Discovered
description: Vin descends into the ancient caverns beneath Kredik Shaw and discovers the Well of Ascension, its power pulsing with accumulated energy.

title: The Power Released
description: At the Well, Vin takes up immense power—then, deceived by Ruin's manipulation of the prophecies, releases it. Something terrible escapes its prison.

title: The Northern Campaign
description: Vin and Elend lead their army to Vetitan, a city being buried by escalating ashfall, to secure one of the Lord Ruler's hidden storage caches.

title: The First Cache
description: Vin opens the Vetitan storage cavern, finding food, supplies, and a metal plate with a cryptic message from the Lord Ruler about the danger he imprisoned.

title: Dividing Forces
description: Vin returns to Luthadel where the army splits: Sazed and Breeze head north to Urteau, while Vin and Elend will march west to Fadrex City.

title: Marching West
description: Vin marches west with Elend's army toward Fadrex City, the ash falling heavier each day as the world deteriorates around them.

title: Arrival at Fadrex
description: The army arrives outside Fadrex City and establishes a siege camp on the ash-covered plains. Obligator Yomen refuses to surrender the city or its cache.

title: The Spy at the Ball
description: Vin infiltrates Fadrex disguised as a noblewoman, attending one of Yomen's balls at Keep Orielle to gather intelligence and seek a diplomatic solution.

title: Second Ball
description: Vin returns to another of Yomen's balls, engaging him directly in philosophical debate about the Lord Ruler's legacy and the growing ashfall.

title: Captured
description: Vin is captured by Yomen's forces and imprisoned in the Canton of Resource, her metals stripped. She is held in a cell with an atium stash she cannot reach.

title: In the Prison
description: Imprisoned and cut off from her metals, Vin contemplates Ruin's influence and the nature of the power she released at the Well.

title: The Cache Revealed
description: Vin discovers the Fadrex storage cache and its critical contents—food, supplies, and a message about the Lord Ruler's true preparations.

title: Escape from Fadrex
description: As koloss attack Fadrex, Vin escapes the city, drawing the Inquisitors away from Elend and the army to protect them.

title: Leading the Hunt
description: Vin flies eastward toward Luthadel with a pack of Inquisitors pursuing her, deliberately drawing Ruin's servants away from Fadrex and Elend.

title: Return to Luthadel
description: Vin arrives in a devastated Luthadel and destroys Kredik Shaw, collapsing the Lord Ruler's palace in a massive display of Allomantic power.

title: Ascending
description: Vin draws upon the mists themselves—Preservation's body—ascending to become a vessel of divine power to fight Ruin directly.

title: The Last Sacrifice
description: Vin sacrifices herself to destroy Ruin, combining Preservation's power with her own life force to annihilate the god of destruction.

title: The Field of Rebirth
description: Vin's body is found in a field of flowers and green grass—the first signs of Sazed's remade world. She lies at peace beside Elend.
